name: TAO 4h Post Expansion Partial Runner v1
edge_mechanism: after a directional expansion proves itself, partial profit is harvested quickly while a core runner is maintained for trend persistence instead of full de-risking too early
thesis: Cycle 73 explicitly wants a post-expansion partial-profit runner structure that keeps a core position for trend persistence. TAO 4h is the best-fit lane because it is the only structurally different asset in the basket with a live trend context capable of rewarding held core exposure. This spec's novelty is not just entry logic; it is management structure as the research variable.
invalidation_condition: If TAO 4h post-expansion entries cannot support a runner after the first partial, or if the setup simply enters too late after the move, the thesis fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A directional expansion proves itself, early profits are harvested, and a remaining core position is allowed to persist if the trend stays intact.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Some continuation setups fail not because entry is wrong but because all exposure is de-risked too early. On a trend-capable lane like TAO, partial-profit plus held core can capture more of the rare persistence move.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: TAO 4h is the best fit because it has enough trend contamination to justify a runner. DOGE and ETH are less suitable for core persistence management.

indicator_role_map.EMA_20: fast structural hold for runner management
indicator_role_map.ATR_14: post-expansion stop sizing and partial spacing

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The entry may occur after too much of the move is spent, leaving the runner to absorb the reversal.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If the first partial works but the runner gives back too much, tighten the runner trail before changing the expansion qualifier.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower count than DOGE reset concepts, but with a management profile where a minority of runner winners matter materially.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: TAO 4h clears density and the runner captures enough net contribution to justify more management-focused refinement.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-12 trades, or a pattern where the runner consistently gives back more than the first partial earns.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: moderate but acceptable because the expansion proof is simpler than prior delayed-retest families

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit lane for testing runner-oriented management after confirmed expansion.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h produces enough expansion entries and the held core contributes meaningful expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h entries are consistently late and the runner becomes a liability.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with a minority of strong runners carrying the result.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same management expression has value in a transition asset, not just a trend asset.
validation_basket.continue_if: DOGE 4h still allows partial-profit plus core persistence without giving everything back.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: DOGE 4h proves the runner concept needs stronger trend contamination.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: More entries but smaller runners than TAO.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to see whether a pure chop lane suppresses the value of runner-based management.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still shows some persistence after expansion.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the management edge depends on trend context.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Sparse runner contributions with many first-partial exits.

long: Trail remaining 50% by 0.95 * ATR_14 while close remains >= EMA_20
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 7 bars if no 1.4 ATR follow-through
short: Trail remaining 50% by 0.95 * ATR_14 while close remains <= EMA_20
Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 7 bars if no 1.4 ATR follow-through
